title: fraxinus oxyphylla var. rostrata
intro: Fraxinus oxyphylla var. rostrata, also known as the Taiwanese ash, is a variety of tree belonging to the Oleaceae family. Traditionally, the plant's bark and leaves have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Fraxinus oxyphylla var. rostrata are said to include relief from fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant's herbal preparations often involve boiling the bark and leaves to make a tea or decoction for various health benefits.
